channel.title: Land of Enchantment Games - News
channel.description: Updates from Land of Enchantment Games

item.title: Dijkstra was right - The Human Computer Interface Part 2
item.description: In my first essay [The Problem of the Human-Computer Interface and Why LLMs are not the Solution](https://enchant.games/?slug=news&article=2026-03-01-the-interface-problem) I attempt to explain why natural languages are terrible for computer programming. It has now come to my attention, that **Dijks

item.title: The Problem of the Human-Computer Interface and Why LLMs are not the Solution
item.description: How computer programming actually works The CPU must be spoken to in a language it understands, that is represented in binary. Hence we have executable files, called binaries, that are computer programs compiled down to cpu-native computer language. And so it follows that we use higher level interme

item.title: Now Who Is The Doomer
item.description: OK, DOOMER? At times I have been accused of dooming too hard on the sad state of tech. This piece aims to be a bit more optimistic. It's so important to me for me and for my friends, to get this issue (on LLMs) right and to communicate a stance with clarity and punch. There is so much FOMO, so much

item.title: Why I care about NOT using AI generative code and HOW I am building a business around NOT using it
item.description: I was challenged by my friend... who is a devops engineer about why I am so strongly opposed to generative AI usage in code, when he said: > "You don't even work in IT anymore, why do you care so much" In this article, I will reply to this question personally, about why I care. The question is a pre

item.title: Happy New Year - Cool Stuff Ahead
item.description: Discord Our Discord is growing in to a tight-nit small group of like minded solo devs (for now), working on our craft, we have many private channels with learning tracks and personal progress trackers where we can sign in and get to work on our games. Currently I have created the following tracks: -

item.title: On Santa Claus
item.description: Answering the Question Plainly In 1897, an eight-year-old girl named Virginia O’Hanlon wrote to a New York newspaper asking whether Santa Claus was real. Some of her friends said no. Her father suggested she write to the paper and ask. Francis P. Church's reply did not beat around the bush, > **Ye

item.title: Let's be gamedevs
item.description: Devops is dead, let's be gamedevs. and... Let's make something beautiful. If the art of code is to be considered. If beauty goodness and truth matter. If integrity of software is anything... Is not the most human thing to do, in an **a**nti **i**ntelligence world, **to buck the system and make indie
